mission_01:
## What You Will Learn

Real-world datasets are messy. Missing values can break models or bias predictions. This mission teaches the first and most critical step in any ML pipeline: data cleaning.

### Key Concepts
- **Missing Values (NaN)**: Occur when data wasn't collected, was lost, or is irrelevant. Most ML algorithms cannot handle NaNs.
- **dropna()**: Removes rows or columns with missing values. We drop rows where the target variable is missing because we cannot train without labels.
- **fillna()**: Fills missing values with a statistic (mean, median, mode). We use the mean for numeric features.
- **Imputation Order**: Always drop target NaNs first, then compute means from remaining data, then fill feature NaNs.

### Why This Matters
Garbage in, garbage out. A model trained on dirty data will produce unreliable predictions no matter how sophisticated the algorithm.
